select_when: The current answer set is too narrow, path-dependent, or anchored on the first plausible idea.
The team can afford small experiments, prototypes, or comparative tests to separate strong variants from weak ones.
Innovation quality depends on broad option generation first and disciplined evidence-based pruning second.
A single perspective is constraining the search space and better alternatives may only appear after deliberate variation.
avoid_when: The team keeps generating options without a selection rule, so experimentation expands but learning and pruning never happen.
Variation is being skipped because people are clinging to the first answer they want to be true.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on divergence-plus-selection discipline rather than on abstract innovation language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader content-strategy and prompt-engineering sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on generate-and-test logic.
open_questions: The source overlaps with experimentation and design thinking, but the operational file stays on disciplined variation followed by selection.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on narrow answer sets, experiments, broad option generation, and evidence-based pruning.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against variation without selection and against fixation on the first answer.

input_type.note: The typed input label compresses the raw conditions around constrained option spaces.

output_type.note: The output label captures the model as a protocol: widen the set, then prune by evidence.

reasoning_types.note: The model relies on exploratory hypothesis generation followed by comparative diagnosis.
